gaps.description: The Bill of Rights is a hollow scaffold with all six articles undefined. It is the constitutional identity of the DIO, but contains no scope of enterprise, no prohibited activities, no core identity, no human authority reservation, no amendment protocol, and no interpretation rules. Arbiter (#190) verifies the BoR at every scope query, but there is nothing substantive to verify — every scope check passes vacuously because no boundaries have been defined.
gaps.mission_ref: BoR Articles I–VI (all marked '[To be defined by human principal]'); MSP 'Relationship to the Bill of Rights' § (MSP is strictly subordinate to BoR)

gaps.description: The Mission Statement Protocol (MSP) is in default-permissive evaluation mode. The bootstrap seed contains no substantive rules — no invariants, no constraints, no safety rules, no organ-specific operating constraints. The second Nomos compliance gate (MSP + genome) passes every Action Proposal by default. The DIO is operating without any machine-actionable operational constitution.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP Rules § ('The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules'); MSP Purpose § (MSP translates BoR into 'precise, machine-actionable objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules')

gaps.description: The Bill of Rights requires human authorship — the BoR document explicitly states authority is 'human-principal-only' and 'No automated system may amend this document.' Yet the BoR has remained a hollow scaffold for 23 days since bootstrap. There is no evidence in the world state that the human principal has engaged with or authored any constitutional content. The entire governance framework is suspended waiting for human input that has not arrived.
gaps.mission_ref: BoR 'Authority: Human principal only. No automated system may amend this document'; MSP 'Relationship to the Bill of Rights' § (when a BoR amendment is needed, Senate-Legislator produces a BoR amendment proposal for the human principal — it does not attempt an MSP amendment as a workaround)

gaps.description: The Radiant dream cycle — the mechanism for memory consolidation, context freshness, and pattern recognition across the DIO's experience — is disabled. No scheduled dream cycles are running, health status is 'never_run', and the last dream cycle recorded was #15 on 2026-04-12. This means the DIO's memory is not being consolidated, context blocks are accumulating without triage, and the pattern-recognition feedback loop envisioned in the architecture is absent.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Genome References ('Genome documents extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules'); Radiant memory architecture (referenced in world state dream_cycle_report entries — memory consolidation is a core organ function designed to run autonomously)

gaps.description: SafeVault backups have experienced persistent and escalating failures since 2026-04-27. The most recent backup (2026-05-03) was killed after ~4860 seconds due to zombie processes from the previous day's run contending for the spine database. NAS mount failures have been continuous since at least 2026-04-27. Script bugs (unbound variable on 2026-04-30) and git sync failures compound the problem. The DIO's data protection integrity is compromised with no MSP rules or genome constraints to enforce backup reliability standards.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Genome References (SafeVault is an organ that should have genome-level operating rules); MSP Purpose § (MSP should define 'invariants, constraints, and safety rules' — data protection invariants are absent)

gaps.description: All 27 Spine-registered organs are exhibiting rapid ALIVE↔DEGRADED state oscillation on approximately 30-second cycles. Every organ transitions to DEGRADED (missed_pong) and recovers (pong_recovered) in lockstep, suggesting a systemic heartbeat issue rather than individual organ failures. This pattern has been continuous since at least 2026-05-03T17:07. The DIO's nervous system is unstable — organ health monitoring cannot distinguish between genuine failures and systemic heartbeat problems when all organs oscillate identically.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Genome References ('Genome documents extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules'); the Spine's organ health monitoring is a structural invariant that should be governed by genome rules for reliability thresholds

gaps.description: All 10 dispatched goals are identical generic placeholders ('Routine maintenance goal #N') with low-to-medium priority. No goal addresses any of the critical gaps identified in the DIO's state — the hollow BoR, default-permissive MSP, disabled dream cycles, or persistent backup failures. The goal dispatch system is running in a placeholder mode that does not reflect the DIO's actual operational needs.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Purpose (MSP should define 'machine-actionable objectives'); the goal dispatch system should align with MSP-defined priorities, but the MSP has no substantive rules to generate goals from

gaps.description: The MSP amendment protocol depends on Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) from Nomos (#200) when Action Proposals are blocked by MSP/genome constraints. Since the MSP has no rules and is in default-permissive mode, no APs are blocked, no PEMs are generated, and the entire constitutional development pipeline (PEM → Legislator draft → review → Rollout activation) is stalled. The governance feedback loop that should drive MSP evolution from operational experience cannot start.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Amendment Protocol ('Amendments to this document originate only from Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) issued by Nomos (#200) when an AP is blocked by current MSP/genome constraints')

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "gap-bor-undefined",
      "summary": "Bill of Rights (BoR) scaffold missing all substantive human-authored constraints — DIO operates without legally operative scope, identity, or prohibited-activity boundaries.",
      "article_ref": "BoR Articles I–VI",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "critical",
      "description": "The BoR remains entirely scaffolded ('[To be defined by human principal]' for all six articles). Article I (Scope of Enterprise), Article II (Prohibited Activities), Article III (Core Identity), Article IV (Human Authority Reservation), Article V (Amendment Protocol), and Article VI (Interpretation Rules) are undefined. Nomos cannot enforce any BoR perimeter; Arbiter (#190) cannot perform its scope gate because there is no hash‑registered, complete BoR to verify. Every proposed action therefore bypasses the first compliance gate, violating the Mission Statement Protocol’s own description of two‑gate adjudication.",
      "rationale": "With an empty BoR, the DIO has no enforceable boundaries. The MSP is strictly subordinate to the BoR, so MSP rules are logically orphaned. Any action taken now could violate future human intent and creates retroactive compliance debt."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-msp-rules-empty",
      "summary": "MSP Rules section is empty, leaving Nomos in default‑permissive mode with no operational constraints, yet goals invoke a non‑existent section.",
      "article_ref": "MSP §Rules (lines: 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules...')",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "description": "The MSP Rules block explicitly states that no substantive rules exist and the DIO operates under default‑permissive MSP evaluation. The recent goals dispatched all reference 'mission_ref': 'MSP §routine-operations', but no section 'routine-operations' exists in the MSP. This is a mismatch that indicates goals are being generated against an imagined or future MSP structure, bypassing any nominal compliance reference. Default‑permissive mode means Nomos (#200) approves any Action Proposal that passes the (non‑existent) BoR gate, effectively placing the DIO in unconstrained operation.",
      "rationale": "Without operational rules, the MSP cannot fulfill its constitutional purpose of translating BoR intent into machine‑actionable constraints. Referencing a phantom section ('routine-operations') obscures the absence of real compliance content and undermines auditability."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-governance-state-empty",
      "summary": "Governance state slice is empty; no versions of BoR or MSP are recorded, indicating Arbiter may be unable to validate current compliance posture.",
      "article_ref": "MSP §Amendment Protocol and BoR footnote ('Hash Registration')",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "medium",
      "description": "The provided governance_state slice is an empty array. The MSP and BoR both mandate hash registration in Graph for version control and Arbiter acceptance: the MSP requires an append‑only 'msp_version' concept with status='active', and the BoR requires a 'bor_version' concept before Arbiter accepts it. An empty state suggests that no version has ever been registered, or the state snapshot is incomplete. Arbiter may be unable to locate the current BoR hash, causing scope queries to fail or fall back to unsafe defaults.",
      "rationale": "Governance state emptiness implies the DIO may be running with Arbiter rejecting or not validating the BoR. Combined with the undefined BoR, this presents a single point of failure in the compliance architecture, leaving the system blind to its own governing documents."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-goal-to-nonexistent-msp-ref",
      "summary": "All recent goals reference 'MSP §routine-operations', a section that does not exist in the active MSP, violating the requirement that goals be grounded in actual compliance provisions.",
      "article_ref": "MSP §Rules (nonexistent section) and MSP 'Genome References' (no routine-operations genome)",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "medium",
      "description": "The 'Recent Goals' list contains 10 dispatch goals all with 'mission_ref': 'MSP §routine-operations'. The MSP contains no such section; the only top‑level headings are Purpose, Rules, Genome References, Relationship to the Bill of Rights, Amendment Protocol, and Version History. There is no genome document extending the MSP for routine operations. This means the goals are not anchored in any auditable compliance source. The anti‑redundancy hint suggests these are routine maintenance goals, but their legal grounding is fictitious, creating a gap between operational tasking and the compliance framework.",
      "rationale": "Even in a permissive mode, mission references should be verifiable. The consistency of this error across all 10 goals suggests a systemic misconfiguration in the goal‑generation pipeline that undermines traceability and may hide genuine compliance drift."
    }
  ]
}

This absence directly causes rapid state flapping (DEGRADED ↔ ALIVE within seconds) across all organs observed in spine-state transitions, leading to operational instability and potential cascading unavailability.",
      "grounding": "MSP §Genome References ('organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints' are absent, 'none at seed'); MSP §Rules ('The bootstrap seed contains no rules').",
      "criticality": "high",
